comments.body: Tangential, but since ~1989 in the US (and  WTO/TRIPS/Berne countries you don’t need a copyright notice. Essentially legally meaningless.
I cannot reproduce your problem using the code in your question. It works OK for me.

Also, you said in the question "in total the footer section just shows the text: Version:". In that case, you are not even seeing the Copyright - text you have shown us in your Thymeleaf template. So, your problem appears to lie elsewhere (not in the code you have shown us).

Sry, (1) "Copyright", was just an example. and (2) of course I can see the word "Copyright - " there. The footer itself works fine. Just the value of "version 1.0.0" (which is used as a dummy String) is missing. If I take a look into the generated html code (e.g. from a browser) there is really no value.

Thank you for clarifying. Sorry, I don't have any suggestions other than doing what I did: Create a new very basic Spring Boot app, using the code in your question, to serve a basic page. That should work (as it did for me). Then change that working code, step-by-step, to match what is not working for you currently. (Someone else may have a better insight, of course.) If your simple app does not work, then you have a minimal reproducible example you can share with us.

It is weird: If I integrate a logger, which prints "getversion is called" inside getVersion(), I can see, that this method is called at every request. I try to create a minimal reproducible example and provide it here.
Does other Thymeleaf render correctly? I'm wondering if there's a general setup situation, such as the th namespace, etc.
Yes it does. But all other replacements are not performed in footer/header-sections and are based on classic Controller/models. Even in my layout.html the ns of thymeleaf is set.

@Stewart - Just a side note: It depends what you specifically mean by "a general setup situation, such as the th namespace" - but Thymeleaf works fine without needing xmlns:th="http://www.thymeleaf.org" in a page. That is just an "incantation" which has "no influence at all in template processing". It's there for other reasons.

@Alex You have said that the @ModelAttribute method IS called for each request, so we're looking for reasons the service_version datum gets lost. Two possible reasons: a) it is overwritten somewhere else in the request, b) you are rendering your view via redirect:, which loses model data. Either that or a typo somewhere?

title: Show global attribute at footer snippet
body:
Using Java 17, Maven 3.9.10, Spring Boot 3.5.3 and the dependency spring-boot-starter-thymeleaf, I would like to create some web pages for a service. I am using the standard approach of Thymeleaf: Having a file templates/layout.html, which contains fragments for page_header, page_footer, and so on. This works fine so far.

In my file templates/layout.html I have the footer-snippet, which represents my footer (shown on all my pages). This snippet should be able to show the value of the version of my service and looks like this:

<footer class="text-center" th:fragment="page_footer">
  <div>
    Copyright - Version: <span th:text="${service_version}"></span>
  </div>
</footer>

I have created a GlobalController class, which should add attributes to all requests:

@ControllerAdvice
public class GlobalController {

    @ModelAttribute("service_version")
    public String getVersion()  {
        return "version 1.0.0";
    }

}

Finally, the service is running and shows the footer in a browser on every route, which is implemented in this service. All these (sub)pages are referencing the footer-snippet, but in total the footer section just shows the text: Copyright - Version: , in other words: the value of version (here: version 1.0.0) is missing and is not replaced/returned.

How can I show the version in the footer?
